records.ety: [L. comprehendere, comprehensum; com- + prehendere to grasp, seize; prae before + hendere (used only in comp.). See Get, and cf. Comprise.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Comprehended; p. pr. & vb. n. Comprehending.]

defs: To contain; to embrace; to include; as, the states comprehended in the Austrian Empire.
quotes: Who hath . . . comprehended the dust of the earth in a measure.

defs: To take in or include by construction or implication; to comprise; to imply.
quotes: Comprehended all in this one word, Discretion.
And if there be any other commandment, it is briefly comprehended in this saying.

defs: To take into the mind; to grasp with the understanding; to apprehend the meaning of; to understand.
quotes: At a loss to comprehend the question.
Great things doeth he, which we can not comprehend.
